name: Westmoreland Bistro Pumpkin Creme Brulee
recipeInstructions:
1. In a heavy saucepan over medium heat, combine the canned pumpkin puree, whipping cream, and vanilla bean halves. Heat to a boil and set aside.
2. In a medium stainless steel bowl (or the top of a double boiler), whisk together the egg yolks and 1/2 cup granulated sugar until pale and thickened, about 6 or 8 minutes. Be careful not to scramble the eggs.
3. Gradually whisk in hot pumpkin mixture into the egg yolk mixture while stirring constantly until well combined.
4. Cook over medium heat for 40 to 50 minutes, until the mixture has the consistency of softly whipped cream. Stir frequently.
5. Add cinnamon, nutmeg, allspice, and ginger; mix well.
6. Pour into 6 custard cups and refrigerate for at least 3 hours or overnight.
7. When ready to serve, sprinkle each custard with about 4 teaspoons granulated sugar. Place under a preheated broiler until the sugar is lightly browned, about 2 to 3 minutes. Serve immediately.
Makes 6 servings
